condition: The cross-fitted outcome-regression estimates are clipped to [-1,1] and the propensity estimate is measurable before applying the q-clipping map, so the clipped AIPW score has envelope at most C/q under ass:bounded-outcome.
reason: The localized VC empirical-process step for the feasible clipped score needs a q^{-1} envelope and q^{-1} variance bound. L2 nuisance rates alone do not bound the score if outcome-regression estimates are unbounded.
standard_or_novel: standard: bounded-outcome nuisance truncation is standard in AIPW/DML analyses, e.g. Chernozhukov et al. 2018 DML and Athey-Wager 2021 policy learning use bounded scores/envelopes.
not_crux: This is a harmless estimator-side truncation preserving L2 rates; it does not assert a regret rate, a margin conversion, or the clipping bias cancellation.

condition: For the feasible-rate exponent there exist a>=0, c>=1/2 and constants C_mu,C_prod such that r_mu,n <= C_mu n^{-a} and r_mu,n r_e,n <= C_prod n^{-c} for all large n.
reason: The frozen ass:nuisance-rate gives rate sequences and the product condition, but a closed-form exponent r_feas requires polynomial exponents for those sequences. Without this, the upper bound is still available as an optimized sequence bound in q, not as n^{-r_feas}.
standard_or_novel: standard: polynomial first-stage rates are the usual way DML/nonparametric nuisance assumptions are stated for exponent calculations.
not_crux: This only parameterizes first-stage estimation quality; it does not assume the policy-regret conclusion or the minimax lower-bound calibration.
